title: SolarEdge Is Showing Early Signs Of A Turnaround
text: SolarEdge Turnaround Analysis. Key Indicators: SEDG has posted 46% year-over-year revenue growth, six straight quarters of gross-margin expansion, and improving free cash flow. SEDG posted three consecutive quarters beating revenue and margin expectations, with 2025 revenue up 31.4% and gross margin rebounding to 16.6%. Operational Improvements: The company is signaling a credible operational turnaround (improving margins, reduced losses, positive free cash flow and stronger cash balance after liability repayment) and is executing product and manufacturing initiatives that should improve long-term margin and U.S. competitiveness. SEDG shifted to US domestic manufacturing, benefiting from IRA tax credits and recovering demand, with Q4 2025 gross margin rebounding to 22%. Recent Performance: The stock has exploded from the high-30s to the high-50s as SolarEdge posted better-than-expected Q1 revenue, a narrower non-GAAP loss of 0.43, and its sixth straight quarter of margin expansion. Investor Caution: However, the company still prints red ink and the Street remains cautious. Recent commentary has framed SolarEdge Technologies as a speculative turnaround story, pointing to ongoing losses, a highly leveraged balance sheet with about 1.75 billion in total liabilities against roughly 427 million in equity, and only recently positive operating cash flow.
